feed.title: SAP Community - Settlement Management
feed.subtitle: Settlement Management Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Accrual reversals before final settlement
entry.content:
Hi All,

We generally reverse the remaining accruals at the end of the CC validity i.e., once CC is fully settled and status is set to "Locked". However, sometimes, business wants to release the accruals much earlier partially without waiting for final settlement to run to release the remaining accrual at the validity end of the contract.

What is the best way forward for it?

Regards

Samier Danish

entry.title: RES1 smaller than accrual REA2 same value as RES1
entry.content:
We are having Z Conditions :

 

Problem is that payout will be smaller than the builded accrual but we won't balance the complete accrual value

Tried to use the REA5 (YEA5) but then the deducted value is double so on this case I will payout 5 € the REA2(YRA4) is the complete amount of 197,40 and the YEA5 is the accrual minus 2*5 € do left in the accrual account 187,40 from my opinion this is not correct. Does anybody have a solution or explanation for me?

entry.title: Two different subtotals for a Rebate condition contract
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have a requirement where for some items Net value should be the rebate basis. Whereas for other items it should be gross price. Is it possible to have two different Rebate basis's for a Contract. In the Settlement data Tab, we can select only on value. Also, let me know how the "Amount Fields - Split Criteria" in the Business volume profile works.

@GauthierVictor@MichaelWerner

entry.title: Actual Costing settlement completed with CKMLMV-039 / CKMLMV-042 warnings after upgrade
entry.content:
After upgrading our SAP S/4HANA system, we started receiving the following warnings during the Costing Cockpit / Settlement step:

  • CKMLMV-039 – Iteration of cycle diverges in currency/valuation

  • CKMLMV-042 – Cycle recalculated after removing critical edges

Key points:

  • Settlement completes successfully

  • No errors 

  • FI postings are balanced

  • Actual prices and cost component split look correct in CKM3N

System setup:

  • Single company code

  • Multiple plants 

Before the upgrade, the same setup did not produce these warnings.

Is this expected behavior after the upgrade, and can these warnings be considered informational only if settlement completes without errors? What causes it and how can i do removed it?

entry.title: Multi‑Payee Scenario – REA2 Condition Type Not Determined
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

In the multi‑payee scenario, when the Sold‑to and Bill‑to are different, the system is not determining the REA2 condition type. However, at the contract level under Condition Usage, it does not show any open accruals — all accruals appear as reversed.
Additionally, at the accounting document level, the accrual reversal is also not reflected.

Request you to please share your inputs.

entry.title: Amount field Group 0S01 - NETWR defaults in the Condition Contract
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am using only 0S02 - BONBA and 0S03-KZWI1 as amount field group fields in the business volume profile. However, 0S01-NETWR is defaulting in the Condition Contract. But in the dropdown, I am seeing only 0S02 and 0S03. Any idea why 0S01 is defaulting into Amount Field Group. How to correct this.

Below is the error message i am getting

Below is the dropdown(Initial)

When I select ZS02 or ZS03 and then hit dropdown, I don't see 0S01 in the dropdown list.

Regards

Hari

entry.title: Error posting reconciliation account on condition contract – Settlement Management
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are facing an issue with a condition contract  (chart of accounts NSCF) in SAP S/4HANA Settlement Management.

Current flow:

  • Provision (Accrual): 709100 / 419211

  • Final Settlement: 419221 / 411xxx

    Issue:
    During the settlement run, SAP throws the following error:

    The account 419221 for company cannot be posted directly Recording with settlement category F (Subsequent item load) Revenue recording procedure: P (via pricing determination) Document type created: AB (Accounting document)

    Additional information:

    • KORS is configured correctly with 419221 as the reconciliation account

      Expected behavior:

      • Settlement should post provision and final settlement:

        • Provision → 709100 / 4192é1

        • Settlement → 419221 / 411xxx

        • 419221 should never be directly posted

Question:

What Settlement Management parameters or account determination settings are required to post 419221 correctly in Settlement, without SAP attempting to post it directly (and causing an error)?

We want to ensure:

  • Provision and Settlement entries are correctly generated

  • Reconciliation account 419221 is used as requested by accounting

  • The postings remain auditable and automatic

    Any guidance or best practices for handling reconciliation accounts in Settlement Management would be greatly appreciated.

    Any guidance or best practices for account determination in Settlement Management for this scenario would be greatly appreciated.

    Thanks,

    Djamila

entry.title: Replacing Allocation Structure in a Work Order
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am trying to find a BAPI or a BADI to change the allocation structure inside a work order (IW32).

I am not able to create a new order type due to business restrictions and need to settle costs using a different allocation structure than the one assigned to the settlement profile.

Requesting help with creating an enhancement for the work order.

Thanks!

Tani

entry.title: Settlement Management - Condition Contract - system external sales documents in Business Volume
entry.content:
Hi community,
one of my customers would like to add system external sales volumes (they are getting these via excel sheets from partner companys) to the sales table VWB2_VBRKVBRP_1 for sales rebates - in addition to the normal S4 internal SD invoices.
Do you have any advice on how I can proceed?
Thanks in advance 
Dan

entry.title: How to get tax to determine based off tax material classification in Settlement Management
entry.content:
From research I understand the 

Tax condition type RETX has reference condition type TTX1 and has access sequence RET1 assigned to it. In the standard content for condition type TTX1, access sequence TTX1 is assigned. The two access sequences (RET1 and TTX1) have one access in common, called Domestic taxes, which contain the following access fields:

Technical name of the conditionField description
ALANDDeparture Country/Region
TAXK1Tax Classification 1-Customer
TAXM1Tax Classification Material

 

I understand by default the tax classification material is set to 1 - How do I get the tax classification to read from the product master data in the settlement management type 0S01 - Sales Rebate ?

entry.title: Impact of Not Settling a Maintenance Order in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I would like to ask for your guidance regarding Maintenance Orders in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

If costs or expenses have been posted to a Maintenance Order, but the order is not settled, what would be the impact on the system?

Specifically, if the order is processed up to Technical Completion (TECO), but Settlement and Business Completion (CLSD) are not performed, could this cause any issues or limitations?
For example:

  • Impact on Month-End or Year-End Closing

  • Open balances remaining on the Maintenance Order

  • Effects on Financial Reporting or Controlling

  • Any system checks or errors during period-end activities

I would appreciate any insights or best practices on whether settlement is mandatory before closing periods, and what potential risks may occur if settlement is not completed.

Thank you very much in advance for your support.

Best regards,
Jessada

entry.title: SAP HANA Rebates: Partner determination for Contract Rebate
entry.content:
Hi Community!

I have a customer in SAP HANA requesting that in Contract Rebates could be by SHIP TO PARTY instead of PI Invoicing Party.  In configuration I see that I can change the partner but I'm not sure if all Invoice documents will be taken into account since Invoice documents have not Ship to party partner.

Could you please help me to understand if I change Contract Rebates partner to SH will be take all Invoice document for Settlement?

 

Regards

Mirtha

entry.title: Reversal of partial settlement incorrect on account assignment for accrual reversal condition
entry.content:
Dears

I am facing a problem that the system does not reverse a partial settlement (or final for that matter) because of unbalanced document.

For a French Sales Org the original document is as Z* condition types copied from standard

Header: 

Item with accruals reversal:

Account assignment for this item

00000001 _________________________________________________________________________
00000001 Tax accounts will be determined for item 000001
00000001 Tax account will be determined for account key ESA
00000001 Tax account 0000015452 found and posted with posting key 50
00000001 _________________________________________________________________________
00000001 Tax accounts will be determined for item 000001
00000001 Tax account will be determined for account key ESE
00000001 Tax account 0000015401 found and posted with posting key 40
00000001 _________________________________________________________________________
00000001 Vendor 0000100248 posted with reconciliation account and posting key 31
00000001 _________________________________________________________________________
00000001 Account determination for condition type ZCA2 ()
00000001 Account determination: acc.key, ZC1 val.grpg code Acct Mod. val.class
00000001 Account 0000048610 will be posted with posting key 50 (transaction ZCA2)
00000001 Account determination for condition type ZCA2 (Commission AccrRev)
00000001 Account determination: acc.key, ZC2 val.grpg code Acct Mod. val.class ZCA2
00000001 Account 0000009696 will be posted with posting key 40 (transaction ZCA2)
00000001 _________________________________________________________________________
00000001 Account determination for condition type ZCS1 (External Commission)
00000001 Account determination: acc.key, val.grpg code Acct Mod. val.class ZCS1
00000001 Account 0000048610 will be posted with posting key 40 (transaction ZCS1)

The reversal document looks like:

Header:

Same item as before:

 

Account assignment for the same item including main messages with the error

FI/CO Interface: Balance in Transaction Currency 28.101,00- (EUR)
Document INT0000001 has status C and is complete
Chart of accounts SGL determined for company code FR01
The posting document is created with transaction RMRP (Incoming invoice)
_________________________________________________________________________
Tax accounts will be determined for item 000001
Tax account will be determined for account key ESA
Tax account 0000015452 found and posted with posting key 40
_________________________________________________________________________
Tax accounts will be determined for item 000001
Tax account will be determined for account key ESE
Tax account 0000015401 found and posted with posting key 50
_________________________________________________________________________
Vendor 0000100248 posted with reconciliation account and posting key 21
_________________________________________________________________________
Account determination for condition type ZCA2 ()
Account determination: acc.key, ZC1 val.grpg code Acct Mod. val.class
Account 0000048610 will be posted with posting key 40 (transaction ZCA2)
Account 0000048610 will be posted with posting key 50 (transaction ZCA2)
Account determination for condition type ZCA2 (Commission AccrRev)
Account determination: acc.key, ZC2 val.grpg code Acct Mod. val.class ZCA2
Account 0000009696 will be posted with posting key 50 (transaction ZCA2)
_________________________________________________________________________
Account determination for condition type ZCS1 (External Commission)
Account determination: acc.key, val.grpg code Acct Mod. val.class ZCS1
Account 0000048610 will be posted with posting key 50 (transaction ZCS1)

As you can see in the reversal - the red marked line is trying to post to the same G/L account with posting Key 50 (besides the 40) and because of this the document is unbalanced.

The settings for the condition contract are shared amongst several sales orgs and if I do the same for a German Sales Org (again same settings - same G/L accounts) the system reverts the document without any issue.

This occurs only in reversal of partial settlement and not in any other cases.

Any idea on how we can see why the system is trying to post with that other account key 50 besides de correct one of 40 (that reverts the original document posting)?

entry.title: Settlement Process for Lump Sum and Slotting Fee using CCM.
entry.content:
Hello Experts,
 
I am currently working on an SAP implementation project for a group of companies operating manufacturing plants and distribution centers across the GCC region.
 
As part of the business model, our products are distributed to customers (stores/malls) within designated territories, and the company is required to manage, accrue, and subsequently settle the following commercial arrangements through credit memos issued to customers:
 
1. Account Opening Fee – Applicable when registering a new customer or opening a new store.( This is a credit Note which company issuing to the Customer in Legacy system through a Custom Fiori App.)
2. Listing Fee – Charged for launching new products into the market.
3. Monthly Shelf Rental Fee – For racks and shelf space allocated to display company products.
4. Promotional Discounts – Seasonal or festival-related trade promotions.
5. Other Fees – Including support fees, renovation contributions, e-commerce fees, flyer fees, etc.
 
These payments need to pay back to Customer as a Credit Memo, which will adjust/reflect in Customer Statement of accounts. 
 
I would greatly appreciate insights from SMEs who have handled similar scenarios, particularly regarding:
 
• Best practice process design
• Recommended SAP solution approach (standard vs. custom)
• Accounting and commercial treatment
• Use of rebate/condition contract management or alternative frameworks.
 
Looking forward to your valuable suggestions and experiences.
 
PS: The Settlement needs to be posted manually through a Credit Note/Credit memo Request. 
 
Thank you in advance for your support.
Best Regards,
Bharath Katukam

entry.title: Error during Portugal Settlement Document Creation – Series Not Registered for Validation Code
entry.content:
System: SAP S/4HANA (Development)

Country: Portugal (PT)

Issue Description:
During the creation of settlement documents for Portugal with digital signature enabled, the system is preventing document posting with an error related to series validation.

Error Message:

Series LPXX is not registered for validation code; posting is not allowed.

Configuration Performed:
The following configurations have been completed:

  1. Number range maintained for Portugal using object GLO_PT_SET.
  2. Number ranges recorded for the digital signature of settlement documents.
  3. The same number range maintained under object W_LFAKTURA.
  4. Country/Region-specific number ranges defined by assigning the same internal number range to country PT.

Observed Behavior:
Despite completing the above configurations, the system throws the validation code error while creating the settlement document and does not allow posting.

Testing Status:
The issue is currently being tested in the development system. Once the issue is resolved and confirmed, the business will proceed with registering the series on the Portuguese Tax Authority portal.

Request:
Kindly advise if any additional configuration or prerequisite is required to resolve this error, or confirm whether series registration and validation code maintenance is mandatory even for development/testing environments.

Thanks and Regards,

Pankaj

entry.title: Field combination not visible when creating new line in business volume criteria
entry.content:
Hi Experts @Lakshmipathi  @GauthierVictor 

Please find the attached screensthots below.

I'm unable to see field combination 0006- customer hierarchy when i'm trying to create new line in the business volume selection criteria in condition contract, i have checked the set field combination and field combination for business volume selection and also specify settlement settings for condition contract types, this combination is present and this field combination is assigned contract type as well, but i'm not able to see this 0006 in standard ones 0S01/0S02 condition contract types and remaining few combinations are visible.

Request you to suggest the missing configuration please.

Settlement Management SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: WB2R111 error
entry.content:
Hello!

I'm running WB2R_SC, our aim is to make the final settlement with the date 31.12.2025

but  WB2R111 - "not all partial settlements could be distributed" error thrown.

Based on the line I’ve marked on the attached image, I’m wondering if the WB2R111 error might be caused by a manual settlement that was create on June.

I’ve noticed that other condition contracts which are currently experiencing the same error also contain manual entries. Could it be that these manual settlements are interfering with the system’s ability to correctly match business volume lines during final settlement?

I’ve checked and found the most suitable OSS note for this error - 3405475 

And I put breakpoint  in the method, mentioned in the OSS note and made debugging  while running  WB2R_SC transaction.  I see system found 1714 lines for partial settlement , however ALL  of their PRCTR values are empty.

by the way, we are using custom Bus. volume table ZSD_VBRKVBRP_6 with split criteria on Z field that's appended on structure KOMLFP. 

Based on the information I’ve provided, how can we run the report successfully and bypass the error?

thans in advance!

entry.title: Store EAN number at settlement document item level for reporting purpose
entry.content:
Hi experts @Lakshmipathi  @GauthierVictor 

We have a requirement to include EAN No. (18 Char) number against each line item in the settlement document. This EAN number comes from material determination setup.

Do we have any standard field where we can store this EAN no. so that we can use this EAN no. for report purpose and other purpose. I have seen a field supplier material number at item level (shown in the attached screenshot) can we use this field, i have tried to put some number in the field in manual rebate process to show you but we are using only automatic settlement only (WB2R_SC). Or do we have any other field we have to store this EAN number, Kindly suggest.

 

Settlement Management  SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Transfer event to be used for transferring the data from material determination to settlement doc it
entry.content:
Hi @Lakshmipathi 

We have a requirement to include EAN No. (18 Char) number against each line item in the settlement document. This EAN number comes from material determination setup.

1. Do we have any standard field where we can store this EAN no. so that we can use this EAN no. for report purpose and other purpose. I have seen a field supplier material number at item level (shown in the attached screenshot) can we use this field, i have tried to put some number in the field in manual rebate process to show you but we are using only automatic settlement only (WB2R_SC). Or do we have any other field we have to store this EAN number.

2. Also, Could you please share the transfer event to be used for transferring the data from material determination/Material master to settlement document item level in the transfer manager.

 

Settlement Management SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Royalties Settlement - Additional Business Volume Table Requirement Class
entry.content:
Dear Experts,
 
We are implementing Royalties Settlement within SAP Condition Contract Management (best practice scope item: 55I)
 
The business requirement is for royalty rebate business volume to reference Royalty Only Paid Invoices from SAP standard business volume table: V_WB2_VBRK_VBRP_4P and the delta accruals business volume to reference the sales order line-item net value of the rebate associated unpaid and paid billing documents. Both rebate and accruals will use the same business volume field selection which is material and utilise same value scales %. Also, the business volume determination will not be using Royalty Recipient partner data as a reference in the billing documents.
 
My proposed configuration approach for the accrual BVB determination:
  1. Create a new Business Volume table for sales order and item data or extend an existing CDS view of unpaid and paid billing docs to include the billed items' assigned sales order item value to capture the accruals business volume amount.
  2. Create a new condition type to capture the accruals' business volume and assign it to the Delta Accruals pricing procedure.
  3. Assign Amount Fields to the accrual BVB condition type and business fieldname in the detailed statement
  4. Set up a selection group and assign to relevant new BVB table.
 
My query is understanding what the Requirements Class is used for in the Business Volume Determination profile configuration. I noticed there is an SAP standard Requirement Class: CL_WB2_BVTAB_REQ_NO_LIFRO_DA that lists Without Royalties Recipient + Delta Accruals in its description, which seems fitting for this requirement. Therefore, for the above delta accrual business volume determination, should this requirement class be assigned in the Business Volume Determination Profile under the sub folder: Additional Business Volumes Tables config to the new sales order item business volume table? For further understanding, this particular requirements class is applicable for delta accrual business volume calculation only and therefore will not impact the royalty rebate business volume calculation?
 
Appreciate your feedback.
 
Many thanks,
Paul
